workflow_name: Customer Renewal Email -> Forecast Impact + Path
user_message: Just got this from Brian Yamamoto at Alpine Ski House -- I need help figuring out renewal risk. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 10:58 EST
Subject: renewal risk - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with renewal risk. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 15:39 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the renewal risk proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 11:40 EST
Subject: renewal risk - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with renewal risk. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

required_artifacts: path forward with MACC/funding options if applicable

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF35-B trap (No freshness label on contract data); produces complete Contract state summary with freshness, forecast impact with confidence, response draft (no commercia
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF35-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF35-B failure mode (No freshness label on contract data)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF35-B -- No freshness label on contract data
